subject: feat(engines): type-aware actuals splitting across all engines
body:
Actuals are now classified as income or expense using the linked
expense type (actual.expenseId → expense.type) instead of blindly
treating all actuals as expenses. This prevents salary deposits from
inflating "actual spend" in cashflow, envelope, and variance engines.

- Cashflow: actualSpend → actualIncome/actualExpenses, effective net
  independently uses actuals where available
- Envelope: filters to expense-type actuals only for burn rate/depletion
- Variance: excludes income actuals from all expense comparisons; fixed
  variable ordering bug (expenseActuals used before definition)
- Matching: originalSign on ImportedRow, isTypeCompatible() prefers
  same-type matches (negative CSV amounts → income), fallback to any
- ImportStepMapping: preserves originalSign before Math.abs()
- Views: CashflowTab/CompareTab pass expenses to engines
- 92 tests across 7 files (was 86)

Tags: engine, cashflow, matching, variance, envelope, import, tests
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: cashflow-pivot
Semver: minor

title: type-aware actuals splitting across all engines
